methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $7.0B total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 9 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: Hadrian
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.6B

private.name: Nimble Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B

data.narrative:
Bear Robotics is a Series C robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $175.8M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of nine same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies, comprising eight private firms (including Hadrian, 1X Technologies, Exotec, and Bedrock Robotics) and one public comparable (ticker 277810.KQ). The peer universe spans valuations from $300M to $10.0B, reflecting substantial heterogeneity in the robotics sector.

Two triangulation methods were applied. The peer-median approach anchors to the median valuation of the nine-company comparable set, which stands at $1.6B. The stage-progression method uses the $7.0B total-raised proxy as a base and applies a 1.7x multiplier typical of Series C-to-next-stage step-ups, yielding $11.9B. These methods produce materially different outputs, reflecting divergent assumptions about market valuation of early-stage robotics capital intensity versus stage-based appreciation patterns.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $1.4B (low) to $13.7B (high), with a median of $6.8B. The wide spread reflects two core uncertainties: whether peer-median multiples appropriately discount for Bear Robotics' specific operational metrics and capital efficiency relative to the comparable set, and whether stage-progression multiples accurately predict Series C-to-beyond valuations in a sector with variable exit outcomes. Data on Bear Robotics' revenue, unit economics, or customer concentration would narrow this range materially.
